items.question: What does in-order traversal of a BST produce?
items.explanation: left → root → right combined with the BST invariant yields ascending order.

items.question: What is the worst-case time complexity of search in an unbalanced BST?
items.explanation: A right-skewed tree (e.g. from sorted inserts) has height n, so search walks all n nodes.

items.question: Which Python library is commonly used to get an ordered (balanced) map?
items.explanation: Provides SortedDict, SortedSet and SortedList with O(log n) operations.

items.question: Which traversal uses a queue?
items.explanation: FIFO order visits nodes level by level from the root.

items.question: Why should you not recurse on a very deep tree in Python?
options: Python's recursion limit defaults to ~1000; deeper trees raise RecursionError
Trees can't be recursed at all
Garbage collection doesn't free recursion frames
items.answer: Python's recursion limit defaults to ~1000; deeper trees raise RecursionError
items.explanation: Use an iterative traversal with an explicit stack/queue for large trees.
